q: How many seats are directly elected in the Lok Sabha?
exp: The Lok Sabha has 543 directly elected seats. The 2 nominated Anglo-Indian seats were abolished by the 104th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2019.

q: In which year was the voting age lowered from 21 to 18 in India?
exp: The 61st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1988 lowered the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

q: What does NOTA stand for?
exp: NOTA = None Of The Above. It was introduced after the Supreme Court's order in 2013.

q: What is EPIC in the context of Indian elections?
exp: EPIC = Elector's Photo Identity Card, commonly called the Voter ID card.

q: Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
exp: The Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) is appointed by the President of India.

q: In which year were EVMs first used in a general election across all constituencies?
exp: EVMs were used nationwide for the first time in the 1999 Lok Sabha General Elections.

q: What is the official website of the Election Commission of India?
exp: eci.gov.in is the official website of the Election Commission of India.

q: What is the Model Code of Conduct?
opts: Guidelines by ECI governing parties and candidates during elections
exp: The MCC is a set of guidelines issued by the ECI that governs the conduct of political parties and candidates.

q: What system does India use to elect Lok Sabha members?
exp: India uses the First-Past-The-Post (FPTP) system, also called Simple Plurality.

q: How many alternative documents does ECI accept on polling day?
exp: ECI accepts 12 alternative photo ID documents if you don't have your Voter ID card.

q: How many seats does the Rajya Sabha have in total?
exp: The Rajya Sabha has 245 seats: 233 elected and 12 nominated by the President.

q: Rajya Sabha members are elected by whom?
opts: By elected members of State Assemblies (MLAs)
exp: Rajya Sabha members are elected by the elected members of State Legislative Assemblies (MLAs).

q: What is the term length of a Rajya Sabha member?
exp: Each Rajya Sabha member serves a 6-year term. It is a permanent house and is never dissolved.

q: A by-election (upchunav) is held when:
opts: A constituency seat falls vacant due to death or resignation
ECI decides to re-test a constituency
A candidate wins by very small margin
exp: A by-election is held to fill a single vacant seat due to death, resignation, or disqualification.

q: Who directly elects the President of India?
opts: An Electoral College of elected MPs and elected MLAs
exp: The President is elected by an Electoral College comprising the elected members of Parliament and State Assemblies.

q: What does MLA stand for?
exp: MLA = Member of Legislative Assembly, elected to the Vidhan Sabha.

q: How many phases did the Lok Sabha General Election 2024 have?
exp: The Lok Sabha General Election 2024 was conducted in 7 phases.

q: What is a 'Hung Parliament'?
opts: When no party wins a clear majority (272+ seats)
exp: A Hung Parliament occurs when no single party or alliance wins 272 or more seats in the Lok Sabha.

q: What is the term for government formed by multiple parties?
exp: A Coalition Government is formed when multiple parties come together to command a majority.

q: Which constitutional article governs President's Rule in a state?
exp: Article 356 allows the President to impose President's Rule in a state.

q: What is the purpose of indelible ink applied to a voter's finger?
exp: Indelible ink is applied to the left index finger to prevent a voter from casting votes more than once.

q: What does VVPAT stand for?
opts: Voter Verification and Poll Audit Trail
Voting Verification and Print Audit Terminal
exp: VVPAT = Voter Verified Paper Audit Trail, a printer device attached to the EVM.

q: Does an EVM have internet or wireless connectivity?
exp: EVMs are completely standalone machines with no WiFi, Bluetooth, or internet connection.

q: What is a 'Mock Poll' conducted before actual voting?
opts: A test of 50 votes cast in front of agents
A poll to decide booth location
exp: A Mock Poll involves casting approx 50 test votes on the EVM to verify it works correctly before actual polling.

q: Who is the Presiding Officer at a polling station?
opts: A government employee appointed by ECI
exp: The Presiding Officer is a government servant temporarily appointed by the ECI to conduct polling at a booth.

q: How many voters are typically assigned per polling booth?
exp: ECI guidelines mandate approximately 1,500 voters per polling booth.

q: Can a voter vote by showing Voter ID if name is NOT in the Roll?
exp: No. Being in the Electoral Roll is mandatory. Even with a Voter ID, you can't vote if your name is missing.

q: What is a Polling Agent?
opts: A representative of a candidate or party
exp: A Polling Agent is an authorised representative of a candidate who observes the polling process.

q: What is 'Booth Capturing'?
opts: Forcibly taking control of a polling booth
exp: Booth capturing is a serious electoral crime involving forcible seizure of a polling station.

q: What is a 'Postal Ballot'?
opts: Votes from service voters or staff on duty
exp: Postal ballots allow service voters and election duty officials to vote by post.

q: The Prime Minister of India is directly elected by:
exp: The PM is appointed by the President based on who commands a majority in the Lok Sabha.

q: What is the primary difference between an MP and an MLA?
opts: MP represents LS (national), MLA represents VS (state)
exp: An MP represents a national constituency, while an MLA represents a state assembly constituency.

q: How is the Chief Minister of a state chosen?
opts: Appointed by Governor based on VS majority
exp: The CM is appointed by the Governor, inviting the leader of the party with a majority in the Vidhan Sabha.

q: What is a 'Floor Test' in Indian politics?
opts: Proving majority support by a vote in the house
exp: A floor test is where a PM/CM must demonstrate they have majority support in the legislature.

q: What is the Anti-Defection Law?
exp: The Anti-Defection Law (10th Schedule) disqualifies members who switch parties without permission.

q: What is the constitutional role of the President of India?
opts: Head of state; executive powers exercised by PM/Cabinet
exp: The President is the head of state, but executive power is exercised by the Council of Ministers.

q: What is the role of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha?
exp: The Speaker presides over Lok Sabha sessions and maintains order.

q: What is a 'Whip' in parliamentary parlance?
opts: Official direction to party members on voting
exp: A Whip is a direction issued by a party to its members on how to vote in the legislature.

q: In India's Parliament, which house is the 'Upper House'?
exp: The Rajya Sabha is the Upper House of India's Parliament.

q: What is the Governor's role in a state?
opts: Constitutional head of state appointed by President
exp: The Governor is the constitutional head of a state, appointed by the President.

q: TRUE or FALSE: If NOTA wins, re-election is ordered.
exp: FALSE. The candidate with the highest number of votes still wins, even if NOTA gets more.

q: TRUE or FALSE: Voting is compulsory in India.
opts: FALSE — it is a right/duty, not obligation
exp: FALSE. Voting is not legally compulsory in India. It is a fundamental right.

q: TRUE or FALSE: Party with most total votes always forms government.
exp: FALSE. It is the number of seats won that determines government formation, not total votes.

q: TRUE or FALSE: EVMs can be hacked wirelessly.
exp: FALSE. EVMs are standalone machines with no wireless connectivity or internet.

q: TRUE or FALSE: You need to be educated to vote.
exp: FALSE. Any Indian citizen 18 or older can vote regardless of education.

q: TRUE or FALSE: Rajya Sabha members are directly elected.
exp: FALSE. Rajya Sabha members are elected by MLAs using proportional representation.

q: TRUE or FALSE: If you miss one election, you lose the right to vote.
exp: FALSE. Missing an election has no legal consequence for your voter registration.

q: TRUE or FALSE: CM is appointed by the Prime Minister.
exp: FALSE. The CM is appointed by the Governor of the state.

q: TRUE or FALSE: You can vote anywhere with Voter ID.
opts: FALSE — only in your enrolled booth
exp: FALSE. You can only vote at the specific polling station where you are enrolled.

q: TRUE or FALSE: ECI is controlled by the central government.
exp: FALSE. ECI is an independent body established under Article 324 of the Constitution.
